name: Busan (South Korea)

colors.symbolism: purity and the openness of the port city
the sea, the harbour, and Busan's identity as Korea's principal port
vitality and the dynamic spirit of Busan's people

description: A white field charged with a stylised emblem of a sailing ship over abstract blue waves, the sail forming the Korean character 부 (bu) in red, with the city's name in stylised letters.
history: Busan (formerly Pusan), Korea's largest port and second city, has been a Special-status Metropolitan City (Gwangyeoksi) since 1995. The current flag and city emblem were adopted on 22 May 1995, with the design centred on the abstracted sail-and-wave motif that evokes Busan's role as Korea's gateway to the world.

features.role: Busan city emblem — sail and wave
features.symbolism: a stylised sailing ship rising over blue waves, with the sail forming the Hangul syllable 부 (bu) in red; together they represent Busan's identity as Korea's principal port and its dynamic, outward-facing character
